reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing immutable conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Generation runs off the main thread in models/smollm2-chat/worker.js via the canonical Transformers.js pipeline("text-generation", "HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-360M-Instruct") + TextStreamer for token streaming. The 'see inside' top-k surface is a REAL extra forward pass (generator.model(inputs) → last-position logits → softmax → top-k) — genuine probabilities, not a mock. Conformance calls-real-inference (source, matches pipeline(/createModelLoader) and loads-clean tested pass; io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The worker auto-picks WebGPU+q4f16 when a real adapter exists and WebAssembly+q8 otherwise (requiresWebGPU:false) — reporting the backend actually used. Either path is real inference — nothing faked. I did not run a fresh live inference in this backfill, so no measured latency/token numbers are claimed.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = streamed continuation tokens from apply_chat_template(messages). The worker streams tokens via TextStreamer (skip_prompt, skip_special_tokens) and returns the final generated_text; the exact templated prompt string the model receives is shown in the see-inside panel, and top-k next-token probabilities are rendered as bars.
rubric.notes: do_sample with temperature/top_p/top_k + repetition_penalty 1.1; an InterruptableStoppingCriteria backs the Stop control.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-360M-Instruct, linked), task = text-generation, backend, and dtype (q4f16 on WebGPU, q8 on the WASM fallback), size ≈360 MB, license Apache-2.0. Conformance declares-model-id/declares-task/declares-quantisation (q4f16)/declares-backend tested pass.
rubric.notes: The worker's MODEL_ID matches the declared id; the backend actually used (webgpu vs wasm) is reported back from the worker in the ready message.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (conformance shared-auto-init-loader source pass); requiresWebGPU:false, so a valid on-device model auto-initialises on WebGPU when an adapter exists and on WebAssembly (q8) otherwise — no Load click needed either way.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced; the model owns transformers-cache (progress_callback drives the loader progress).

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error announced in role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass). Worker errors surface on the page (errors-on-the-page source pass). Because there is a genuine WASM path, no-GPU devices still run for real (auto-picked), and an honest opt-in WASM note/needs-WebGPU explanation is shown — never a blank panel. webgpu-capability-honest (probe) is in the suite.
rubric.notes: A real WebGPU probe (adapter must resolve) picks the device; navigator.gpu existing alone is not treated as sufficient.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: system-prompt textarea, temperature/top_p/top_k/max-tokens sliders (reflect live), the composer, Send, Stop (InterruptableStoppingCriteria), Clear, and sample chips; the see-inside top-k panel is driven by a real forward pass.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is a manual-evidenced conformance assertion.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — bubbles are filled from streamed TextStreamer tokens; top-k bars are the softmax of real logits. no-fake-output is a manual-evidenced assertion.

Conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt (script) and live-status-region (dom, [role=status][aria-live]) tested pass at build. The status line is role=status aria-live=polite; the chat log is aria-live. HOWEVER the responsive pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets pending agent verification (support=needs-review, automatedNotes: 9 on desktop / 5 on mobile — mostly the range-slider thumbs and small secondary buttons), below this repo's ~44px tap-target bar.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (public/styles.css). The sub-24px flag is the real open a11y item on this demo.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid: controls flex-wrap, the chat/transcript log is intrinsically sized (min/max-block-size + overflow-y), textareas/canvas use inline-size, and the see-inside stat grid is auto-fit minmax. Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite. support=needs-review on both classes: the automated pre-scan reported 9 sub-24px target(s) desktop / 5 mobile awaiting an agent matrix pass — no live matrix was run in this backfill. This build is ≈360 MB — small enough that the WASM fallback keeps it usable on no-GPU devices.
rubric.notes: I did NOT run responsive-check in this retrospective pass, so I do not claim a measured 0px-overflow result; the honest status is needs-review with the sub-24px tap-target flag.

rubric.evidence: Inference + the top-k forward pass run in the Web Worker; the main thread only appends streamed tokens and updates a caret (guarded by prefers-reduced-motion). Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source) tested pass; inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The top-k softmax loop over the vocab runs in the worker, off the UI thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker streaming tokens
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread; avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — pipeline inference + the see-inside top-k forward pass both run in a dedicated module Worker; the UI thread only paints streamed tokens.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread pipeline + TextStreamer; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks main thread responsiveness
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up heavy synchronous processing so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — output is streamed token-by-token from the worker; the vocab-wide softmax for top-k also runs in the worker, never blocking the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: TextStreamer callback posts per-token; topk() softmax loop in worker.js

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keyboard operability.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live announcements + accessible button names (buttons-have-names pass). EXCEPTION pending: sub-24px tap targets flagged for an agent matrix pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region + buttons-have-names pass; support=needs-review sub-24px note

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls flex-wrap, the transcript is intrinsically sized with overflow-y, the composer textarea uses resize:vertical with a min-block-size, and the top-k rows use a grid template — no fixed widths forcing overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: QWEN_CSS flex-wrap + max-block-size transcript + grid top-k rows

openQuestions: Verify and enlarge the 9 desktop / 5 mobile sub-24px tap targets (mainly range-slider thumbs) to ~44px, then flip support from needs-review to ok with a real matrix pass.
The composer textareas use resize:vertical rather than field-sizing:content — worth considering field-sizing (as the WebLLM demos use) for a smoother auto-fit composer.

followUpGoals.goal: Run scripts/responsive-check.mjs at 360×740 and 1280×800, enlarge the flagged sub-24px tap targets, and record support=ok per class.
Consider adopting field-sizing:content on the composer/system-prompt textareas for parity with the WebLLM chat demos.

summary: Revision-1 retrospective critique backfill for smollm2-chat (HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-360M-Instruct, text-generation, Transformers.js, q4f16 on WebGPU with a real q8 WASM fallback, ≈360 MB, Apache-2.0). Evidence grounded in code inspection + the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, shared-auto-init-loader, inference-off-main-thread, declares-*, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass). Real streaming chat via pipeline()+TextStreamer in a worker, with an honest see-inside top-k from a genuine extra forward pass (softmax of real logits). requiresWebGPU:false → auto-picks WebGPU/WASM, so it runs for real on no-GPU devices too. GENUINE OPEN ITEM: support=needs-review with 9/5 sub-24px tap targets pending an agent matrix pass; no fresh live-inference numbers claimed.
